marketState: neutral, with a hot-weather short-term bounce sitting inside a still-bearish medium-term backdrop
summary: Front-month gas ($2.939) is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.917 now, rising to $3.02 by +21D) with no band-edge stretch, so there is no strong valuation edge in either direction. Seasonality is flagged Low reliability this week (59pp spread) but shows price 13.7% below its typical YTD path and only a 38% chance of being higher in 4 weeks, a mild medium-term headwind reinforced by a storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y) that grew more than forecast (+72 Bcf surprise, bearish). Working against that is very hot near-term weather demand (+19.7% 1-7D, +15.3% 8-16D vs 5y, both near the top of their range), which is fueling an intraday breakout (5m/15m RSI ~67, price above upper Bollinger) even though daily/weekly trend, EMAs and MACD remain bearish. Managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of the last 5 years, so positioning is not extreme in either direction. Net: near-term weather can support a bounce, but the bigger-picture storage overhang and soft seasonality argue against chasing it far or leaning long-term bullish yet.

longTerm.rationale: Signals conflict and the seasonality read is explicitly low-reliability this week: storage is well above the 5y average with a bearish build surprise and price is running 13.7% below its typical seasonal path, but weather demand is hot and fair value drifts up to $3.02 by +21D while COT shorts are only mid-percentile (35%), leaving no crowded position to fade.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback toward the lower fair-value band (~$2.55-2.70) to buy ahead of the seasonally strong Sep/Oct window, or (b) the storage deviation vs 5y beginning to narrow / a bearish build surprise turning to a draw surprise, or (c) the daily trend flipping (EMA50>200 and MACD turning positive on the 1d/1w) to confirm a base is in.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather demand is running well above normal (+19.7% 1-7D, +15.3% 8-16D, both near the top of their 5y range) and the futures curve shows the front slightly backwardated to Sep/Oct, consistent with prompt tightness; price is also fairly valued with no downside stretch to worry about. Caveat: 5m/15m RSI near 67 with price already above the upper Bollinger band signals the pop is a bit extended, so this is a small, tactical long rather than a high-conviction breakout chase.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back below $2.82 (loss of the recent range low) or a cooling shift in the 1-16 day demand forecast back toward/below the 5y normal would invalidate the setup.

keyDrivers: 1-16 day weather demand +15-20% vs 5y, near the top of its range — bullish near-term
Storage +184 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise — bearish overhang
Price running 13.7% below its typical seasonal path; seasonality reliability is Low this week
Front price essentially at fair value at every horizon (no band-edge mispricing)
Managed-money net short but only 35th percentile of the last 5y — not a crowded position
risks: Weather forecasts can flip quickly, removing the main near-term bullish driver
Daily/weekly technicals (EMA50<200, negative MACD) still point down, so any bounce fights the prevailing trend
Low seasonality reliability (59pp 10-90 spread) means the historical seasonal edge could easily fail this year
